title: ActionResult
description: The device→brain result of dispatching one Action, sent back UP over the 8765 channel. Mirrors the Android ActuatorResult{success, detail} (overlay/Actuators.kt) plus an optional error kind and an optional fresh observation so the loop can act on the post-action screen state without a separate round-trip. `detail` carries ONLY the actuator's own non-sensitive phrase (e.g. 'tapped node[...]', 'opened maps', 'user declined credential entry') — never node screen text or typed text.

msg.$comment: MESSAGE-KIND discriminator (I2). Const 'action_result' for every result frame — the SEPARATE key from any action-variant `type`, so the message kind and the action variant never collide on the wire.
msg.description: Message kind — always 'action_result' for a device→brain result frame (vs 'observation' / 'action').

success.description: ActuatorResult.success — whether the action was performed. A declined confirm-gate or credential handoff returns success=false with a benign detail (not an error).

detail.description: ActuatorResult.detail — a fixed, non-sensitive human/model-readable phrase. Success e.g. 'tapped node[id]', 'set text on node[id]', 'opened maps', 'user entered their credential'. Non-error failure e.g. 'user declined', 'user declined credential entry'.

error.description: OPTIONAL structured error kind (present only when success=false for a genuine failure, not a user decline). Maps the device-side failure ('accessibility service not enabled' → not_enabled, 'node N not found' → node_not_found, 'unknown phone action' → unknown_action, '<key> required'/'invalid url' → invalid_argument, gesture/intent launch failure → dispatch_failed) plus the loop-level transport errors (no_device / lost_contact / timeout from the M2 adaptive-timeout layer). `not_wired` is the M0 SCAFFOLD state: the /action endpoint acknowledges a well-formed action but the actuator dispatch (M1) / cloud loop (M2) is not yet bound — it is removed once real dispatch lands.

observation.description: OPTIONAL post-action observation (fresh screen state), so the frontier loop can re-observe without a separate request. Omitted when the caller will pull the next observation independently.
